Quantum Computing for Hydrology and Water Resources

HydroQuantum

HydroQuantum is a platform advancing the application of quantum computing in hydrology and water resources. It develops and integrates quantum algorithms and quantum-enhanced machine learning to improve modeling, prediction, optimization, and uncertainty quantification in complex water systems. All codes, publications, and training materials are freely available. We welcome collaboration and discussion on quantum-related modeling and simulations in hydrology and water resources.

Please feel free to contact us at samadi@clemson.edu if you are interested.

Explore Projects View Publications

Advancing quantum computing applications in hydrology, irrigation, water quality, and large-scale environmental modeling.
